info.title: Customer Management API
info.description: APIs for the creation and management of customer information

post.description:
Creates a new **Billing Group** within the specified Customer.
A billing group defines how billing, invoicing, charges, and fees are
organised for one or more Legal Entities belonging to the customer.

## Purpose
Billing Groups act as logical containers that determine:
- How billing rules are applied
- Which Legal Entities/Products participate in a given billing arrangement
- How charges and usage are aggregated
- How invoices are grouped

They provide a structural layer between Legal Entities and billing logic,
enabling more complex arrangements such as multi-entity billing,
consolidated invoicing, or separate billing streams within the same customer.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Newly created Billing Groups start with:
- `draft` version = `0`
- `submitted` version = `0`
- `approved` version = `0`

The provided payload populates the **draft** slot.

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
- The Billing Group is immediately **submitted for evaluation**.

If `submit` is omitted or false:
- The caller may submit the Billing Group later via the dedicated submit
  endpoint.

Evaluation may return:
- Missing configuration
- Inconsistent or invalid billing relationships
- Document or data requirements

Once all findings are resolved and re-evaluated, the approved version is
updated and used across billing processes.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Requires a valid JWT with the `CREATE_BILLING_GROUP` permission.
- The referenced customer must exist.
- The request body is validated; errors are returned in the response.

## Result
Returns the persisted Billing Group as an `EntityResponse` with a `201 Created`
status code.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Billing Groups** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing billing arrangements before selecting a specific billing
group for editing, submission, or association to legal entities/products.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided `customer_id`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned.  Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted.  Specified as a csv list of fields.  See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data.  Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data.  Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Billing Group** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single billing
group prior to updates or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Returns 404 when the billing group is not found or not visible to caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<BillingGroup>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Billing Group

put.description:
Updates an existing **Billing Group** draft.

## Purpose
Refine billing configuration before submission or after evaluation feedback
(e.g., invoice grouping, charging rules, entity participation).

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_BLLING_GROUP`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies changes to the submitted slot and triggers
  evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Billing Group** within the specified customer.

## Purpose
Remove erroneous or obsolete billing arrangements prior to activation or
usage.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation is scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Billing Group

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Billing Group** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Validate billing configuration (associations, charge rules, invoicing logic)
before it is applied for live billing.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged
  until evaluation/apply completes.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the billing group for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Billing Group**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes) even
without new draft submissions.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Contacts** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing contacts before selecting one for update, submission, or
association to legal entities and workflows.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_CONTACT`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided `customer_id`.

post.description:
Creates a new **Contact** for the specified Customer.
A contact represents an individual associated with the customer or its
underlying legal entities, typically used for communication, operational
coordination, and compliance-related processes.

## Purpose
Contacts provide a structured, versioned record of:
- Key individuals linked to the customer
- Their roles, responsibilities, and relationship to the business
- Points of communication for operational, billing, or compliance workflows

Contacts are an important supporting entity, enabling downstream processes
such as onboarding, risk evaluation, customer servicing, and regulatory
checks.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Newly created contacts follow the standard entity lifecycle:
- **draft**, **submitted**, and **approved** version numbers all begin at `0`
- The draft copy is populated with the data provided in the request

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft copy is also written to the submitted slot
- The contact is immediately **submitted for evaluation**

If omitted:
- The contact remains in draft form until explicitly submitted
  via the submit endpoint

The **approved** slot is updated only once evaluation completes and the apply
workflow has run successfully.

## Submission & Evaluation
During evaluation the system may identify:
- Missing or invalid personal details
- Required supporting information
- Policy or compliance checks triggered by the data

Any findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before the
contact can be approved.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Caller must have `CREATE_CONTACT` permission for the target customer.
- A valid JWT from the authorisation server must accompany the request.
- Payload validation errors are returned with structured details.

## Result
Returns the newly created Contact wrapped in `EntityResponse` with a
**201 Created** response.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the contact should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Contact** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single contact
before editing or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_CONTACT`.
- Returns 404 when the contact is not found or not visible to caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<Contact>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Contact

Updates an existing **Contact** draft.

## Purpose
Refine contact details (roles, communication info, responsibilities) before
submission or after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_CONTACT`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies the change to the submitted slot and
  triggers evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the contact change should be immediately submitted

requestBody.description: Versioned update of the Contact details.

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Contact** within the specified customer.

## Purpose
Remove erroneous or obsolete contact records prior to activation or use in
workflows.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_CONTACT`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Contact

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Legal Entities** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing legal entities, typically to show summaries in dashboards
or to locate a specific entity before drilling into details.

## Behaviour
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes to the supplied `customer_id`; caller must have `READ_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns lightweight `EntityResponse<LegalEntity>` entries.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned.  Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted.  Specified as a csv list of fields.  See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data.  Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data.  Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

post.description:
Creates a new **Legal Entity**, the core business object representing an organisation, its ownership, operational details, and beneficial parties.

## Purpose
A Legal Entity is the foundation of the merchant lifecycle.
It provides the authoritative record of:
- Business identity and structure.
- Ownership and persons of significant control.
- Operational characteristics.
- Relationships to other legal entities (for complex hierarchies).

## Typical Use Cases
- Onboarding a new merchant or partner.
- Registering a new business entity within an existing group.
- Defining hierarchical corporate structures.

## Submission & Evaluation
After creation, the Legal Entity may be **submitted for approval**.
Submission triggers the evaluation workflow, which may identify:
- Missing or inconsistent business information.
- Required supporting documents.
- Compliance or risk-related questions.

Any findings are returned to the client. Once addressed and re-evaluated,
the Legal Entity can be **applied**, making its data active across all
downstream systems.

## Related Domains
Legal Entities link to several other resource domains:
- Products.
- Trading locations.
- Bank accounts.

These are managed by their respective APIs but form part of the overall
entity lifecycle.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the legal entity should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Legal Entity** by its ID.

## Purpose
Fetch the current representation of a single legal entity to display or
validate data prior to updates/submission.

## Behaviour
- Enforces customer scoping and `READ_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns 404 when the entity does not exist or is not visible to the caller.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

put.description:
Updates an existing **Legal Entity** draft (business data).

## Purpose
Apply changes to core legal-entity attributes before submission or as part
of remediation after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Supports optional immediate submission via `submit` query flag.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `version` inside `EntityUpdateRequest`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the legal entity change should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a **Legal Entity** (logical delete).

## Purpose
Retire an entity that was created in error or is no longer needed prior to
activation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns 200 on success, 404 if the entity cannot be found.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Legal Entity** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Trigger the compliance/risk evaluation workflow that validates completeness,
required documents, and business rules.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- On acceptance, copies draft to submitted slot and starts evaluation.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers evaluation for a **Legal Entity** without making data changes.

## Purpose
Re-run downstream evaluations after external changes (e.g., document status
updates) or to refresh derived state.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Fires the same evaluation pipeline used post-submission.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Bank Accounts** for a legal entity.

## Purpose
Discover configured payout/settlement destinations before selecting or
editing a specific bank account.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided `customer_id` and `legal_entity_id`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned.  Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted.  Specified as a csv list of fields.  See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data.  Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: The response data is an array of details about each entity

post.description:
Creates a new **Bank Account** for a given Legal Entity within a Customer.
Bank accounts capture settlement and payout details used across downstream
payment and billing processes.

## Purpose
A Bank Account belongs to:
- A **Customer** (top-level customer container), and
- A **Legal Entity** (the business that owns/uses the bank account).

It provides the canonical record of where funds are settled and how payouts
are routed for that legal entity.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Bank accounts participate in the same draft / submitted / approved lifecycle
as other configurable entities:
- On creation, the **draft**, **submitted**, and **approved** version numbers
  are all initialised to `0`.
- The draft slot is always updated with the provided details.
- The approved slot is only updated once evaluation completes successfully
  and the apply workflow has run.

The `legal_entity_id` is taken from the URL and stored on the bank account;
this association **cannot be changed** after creation.

## Submission & Evaluation
If the `submit` query flag is set:
- The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
- The bank account is immediately **submitted for evaluation**.

If `submit` is not set:
- The caller is expected to submit the bank account later via the
  dedicated submit endpoint.
- Bank account submission may also be triggered from a Legal Entity workflow
  (for example, as part of a post-apply process when the Legal Entity is
  approved).

During evaluation, any issues, missing data, or document requirements are
surfaced back to the caller. Once these are resolved and evaluation
completes, the approved copy is updated and used across all systems.

## Validation & Authorisation
- The referenced Legal Entity **must exist** at the time of creation.
- The caller must be authorised to create bank accounts for the given
  customer and legal entity (via a signed JWT from the authorisation server
  with the required permissions).
- The request payload must be valid; any validation failures are returned in
  the response with details of the errors.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the bank account should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Bank Account** by ID within a legal entity.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single bank
account prior to updates or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Returns 404 when the bank account is not found or not visible to caller.
- Includes status/version details in `EntityResponse<BankAccount>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the bank account

200.description: The response data contains the currently saved version of the resource along with details about the currently submitted and approved versions.  In addition the response provides information about any errors or requests that the most recently completed approval process has generated

put.description:
Updates an existing **Bank Account** draft.

## Purpose
Correct or enrich payout/settlement details before submission or after
evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies the change to the submitted slot and
  triggers evaluation.
- Scopes authorisation to the owning customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the bank account change should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Bank Account** for the specified legal entity.

## Purpose
Remove erroneous or obsolete bank account definitions prior to activation
or payout routing.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation is scoped to the customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the bank account

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Bank Account** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Trigger validation of payout/settlement details and compliance checks prior
to activation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged
  until evaluation and apply complete.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation is scoped to the customer and legal entity provided.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the bank account for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers a re-evaluation of the specified **Bank Account**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes) even
when no new draft data has been submitted.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_BANK_ACCOUNT`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Product Agreements** for a legal
entity.

## Purpose
Discover configured product agreements before selecting one for update,
submission, or evaluation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for which the products should be returned

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned.  Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted.  Specified as a csv list of fields.  See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data.  Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data.  Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

post.description:
Creates a new **Product Agreement** for the specified Customer and (typically)
associated Legal Entity.
A product agreement defines which products or services the customer intends
to use, together with configuration, eligibility information, and any
product-specific operational requirements.

## Purpose
Product Agreements serve as the core artefacts for enabling and configuring
products within the platform.
They describe:
- Which products a customer is onboarding to
- The configuration and parameters for each product
- Product-specific requirements (documents, checks, capabilities)
- The lifecycle and state of each activated product

They act as a contract-level definition between the platform and the
customer's legal entity.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Product Agreements follow the standard entity lifecycle:
- `draft`, `submitted`, and `approved` version numbers all begin at `0`
- The **draft** copy is populated using the request body

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft is also copied to the submitted slot
- The Product Agreement is **immediately submitted for evaluation**

If not submitted:
- The agreement remains in draft form until explicitly submitted via the
  submit endpoint or by workflows that automatically trigger submission

The **approved** slot is only updated once evaluation completes and the
apply workflow has been successfully executed.

## Submission & Evaluation
Upon submission, a Product Agreement is evaluated using the product's
definition and configured workflow logic. Evaluation may identify:
- Missing or invalid configuration fields
- Inconsistent product selections
- Required documents or compliance checks
- Product dependency issues (e.g., prerequisites not met)

Findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before approval
can occur.

## Relationships
Product Agreements typically relate to:
- A **Legal Entity** that the product applies to
- One or more **Product Definitions** that specify rules and capabilities
- Associated configuration entities (billing, locations, bank accounts)

These links allow workflows to determine what configuration must exist
before the agreement can be approved.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Caller must present a valid JWT containing `CREATE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT` or
  equivalent permissions for the target customer.
- The referenced customer must exist.
- Request payload must pass validation; structured error responses are
  returned for invalid data.

## Result
Returns the newly created Product Agreement wrapped in an
`EntityResponse` with a **201 Created** status.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the product agreement should be immediately submitted

requestBody.description: Details of the new product to be created

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Product Agreement** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for an agreement before
editing or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Returns 404 when the agreement is not found or not visible to the caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<ProductAgreement>`.

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for which the products should be returned

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the product agreement

put.description:
Updates an existing **Product Agreement** draft.

## Purpose
Refine product configuration before submission or after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies the change to the submitted slot and
  triggers evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the product agreement change should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Product Agreement**.

## Purpose
Remove obsolete or erroneous agreements prior to activation or usage in
downstream flows.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the product agreement

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Product Agreement** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Trigger validation of product configuration and eligibility prior to
activation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Copies draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until
  evaluation/apply completes.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Product Agreement**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes)
without submitting new draft content.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_PRODUCT_AGREEMENT`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Trading Locations** for a legal
entity.

## Purpose
Discover configured operating locations before selecting one for update,
submission, or evaluation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for which the trading locations should be returned

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned.  Idisplayrovided 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted.  Specified as a csv list of fields.  See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data.  Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data.  Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

post.description:
Creates a new **Trading Location** for the specified Customer and Legal Entity.
A trading location represents a place (physical or online) where the
business conducts trading activity.

## Purpose
Trading locations provide structured, versioned records of:
- Where the business operates (shops, sites, online channels, etc.)
- How those locations are identified and described operationally
- Which Legal Entity is responsible for activity at each location

They are a key part of onboarding and risk evaluation, and are used by
downstream systems for routing, reporting, and compliance checks.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Trading locations follow the standard entity lifecycle:
- `draft`, `submitted`, and `approved` version numbers all begin at `0`.
- The **draft** copy is populated with the data from the request body.

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
- The trading location is immediately **submitted for evaluation**.

If `submit` is not set:
- The trading location remains in draft form until explicitly submitted via
  the submit endpoint or via a Legal Entity–driven workflow.

The **approved** slot is only updated once evaluation completes successfully
and the apply workflow has run.

The trading location is created with its `legal_entity_id` set from the URL;
this association **cannot be changed** after creation.

## Submission & Evaluation
Submission may be triggered:
- Directly by calling this endpoint with `submit=true`, or
- Indirectly from a Legal Entity workflow that includes one or more trading
  locations as part of a wider change set.

During evaluation, the system may identify:
- Missing or invalid address / location details
- Conflicts or inconsistencies with the Legal Entity configuration
- Additional document or data requirements

Any findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before the
approved copy is updated.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Caller must have permission to `CREATE_TRADING_LOCATION` for the target
  customer and legal entity.
- Authorisation is provided via a signed JWT issued by the authorisation
  server.
- The request payload must pass validation; failures are returned with
  structured error details.

## Result
Returns the newly created Trading Location wrapped in an
`EntityResponse<TradingLocation>` with a **201 Created** status.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the trading location should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Trading Location** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a location before
editing or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Returns 404 when the location is not found or not visible to the caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<TradingLocation>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the trading location

put.description:
Updates an existing **Trading Location** draft.

## Purpose
Refine location details before submission or after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies the change to the submitted slot and
  triggers evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the trading location change should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Trading Location**.

## Purpose
Remove obsolete or erroneous locations prior to activation or usage in
downstream processes.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the trading location

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Trading Location** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Trigger validation of the location (address, operations, associations) prior
to activation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Copies draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged until
  evaluation/apply completes.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer and legal entity.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: legal_entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for which the trading locations should be returned

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the trading location for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Trading Location**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external data changes)
without submitting new draft content.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_TRADING_LOCATION`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer/legal entity context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.
